transcript: Hi guys and welcome back to another video. So I've got 10 plyometric exercises for you today to improve your explosive speed. And the best thing about these exercises, you don't need any equipment or much room whatsoever so you can do these at home at an open pitch wherever you like and you can still improve your running speed, your change of direction, your jumping power, all of those movements in a match that require explosive power. So we've got 10 in total total let's get into the first exercise exercise one is jump squats so we're taking a normal squat which is used to build the hamstrings and the quads and we'll add in an explosive element by jumping up into the air and as you can see i'm cushioning my landing by bending the knees and i'm putting my hands on my shoulders just to take away the momentum of my arms which would usually use when jumping. Taking away this momentum puts even more stress on the legs so you're building even more explosiveness and also forces you to engage the core. Exercise two is jump lunges so this is when we bend both legs 90 degrees one front the other, and then we explode up into the air by straightening both of our legs, and then we land with our opposite foot in front. And as you can see, cushioning my landing once again by bending the knee as I make impact with the ground. And this time I'm using both of my arms for balance, and they're bent 90 degrees as well, and as my right comes forward my left leg comes forward and vice versa exercise three is lateral balance so this time we're working one leg at a time bounding from side to side so all our weight is going on a single leg so really having to engage those hamstrings quads and calves and then our core has to be engaged as well to balance and we're using those arms as well so this exercise is really going to improve your change of direction making you more explosive off the mark because you're loading one leg so when you change directions all the weight goes on one side so we're replicating that in this exercise so if you do this often you're going to see yourself change the direction a lot quicker number four is a skip so we're on our toes bouncing and then we're driving one knee up at a time and as we drive one knee up the opposite arm drives out in front as well so this is working on those lower legs as we hop on the spot you're going to feel it burning your calves but also engaging those hip flexors which are really important for explosive speed exercise five is reverse lunge knee drive so we're performing a reverse lunge so our foot goes behind us bending both knees 90 degrees and then as we push up we also drive our knee of our opposite leg into the air as well so we're working all of those leg muscles but also the hip flexors as well and as you can see driving the opposite arm with the opposite knee as i'm driving upwards this is a really tiring one i like to do five on each side and then three sets all together exercise six is tuck jumps this time we're using our arms as momentum and then we're lifting both hip flexors so really getting a lot of hip flexor work here and we're bringing both knees up 90 degrees parallel with our hips so nice jumps and as you can see i crouch forward so having to engage that core as well a great exercise for building jumping power and overall explosiveness exercise seven is high knees so this is a great exercise for working on not only developing your running technique, but it will increase your running speed if you do this consistently because you're forcing yourself to synchronize those arm and knee movements. So as you can see, as my left arm drives, my right knee drives and vice versa. And if you work on this, it's going to make your running technique a lot more efficient, a lot more explosive and overall increase your running speed exercise eight is heel flicks so we're bringing up those heels behind us towards ourselves which really isolates the hamstring muscles to perform that leg flexion you're really engaging the fast twitch fibers in your hamstrings which are going to make you overall more explosive when you're running and once again remaining on those toes getting in a rhythm and trying to coordinate those arm movements with our leg flexion as well exercise nine is kneeling jumps so we're starting in a kneeling position then swinging our arms through and using that momentum to jump up off of our knees and land onto our feet really forcing you to engage those hip flexors they have to be really explosive to be able to swing those legs up and engaging that core as much as possible the combination of your hip flexors and your core should be enough to give you that momentum and power to jump back onto your feet. Really good movement for developing your explosive capabilities. It might take a little bit of practice to get this one but when you do you're going to see a huge translation into your performance on the pitch in your running speed, your jumping power and all of those movements that require maximal force in a short period of time. Number 10 is calf jumps. So this looks a little bit similar to the jump squats we performed in exercise number one, but as you can see, we're not bending the knees anywhere near as much much so we're really trying to load the lower legs instead of the quadriceps and the hamstrings so really working those calf muscles trying to be explosive pointing those toes as i jump up in the air we're using a slight bend just to absorb the impact and give you a little bit of momentum but all the load is really going on those calf muscles so we can make those more explosive it's going to improve your running speed but also your jumping capabilities as well so there we go guys those are the 10 exercises that you can complete anywhere just using your own body weight but you're going to dramatically increase your running speed change the direction jumping power all of those movements that require maximum force in a short space of time and if you want to do a full workout try each of these exercises for 10 reps all the way through one through 10 and then complete them three times modify them to your own fitness levels but if you do these a few times a week with a day of rest in between i'd recommend three days a week you're going to see huge improvements in all of those areas but if you did enjoy today's video make sure you smash that like button hit the subscribe button for weekly training videos and i will if you did enjoy today's video make sure you smash that like button hit the subscribe button for weekly training videos and i will see you next time.
